timeline.summary: Triaged as SEMI_URGENT. Patient presenting with persistent abdominal discomfort and mild fever. Needs evaluation for possible infection.

details.triage_reasoning: Patient presents with persistent abdominal discomfort, mild fever, dark urine, and recent appetite decrease, indicating a possible underlying infection or gallbladder issue, necessitating timely evaluation.
details.notes: Patient has a history of gallstones, which adds to the concern.
details.context_for_next: Evaluation for possible infection or complications related to gallstones.
details.viewer_output: Triaged as SEMI_URGENT. Patient presenting with persistent abdominal discomfort and mild fever. Needs evaluation for possible infection.

timeline.summary: Ordering abdominal ultrasound due to symptoms indicating potential gallbladder complications. Found 4 relevant past cases suggesting this approach.

details.context_for_next: Patient presents with semi-urgent symptoms concerning possible infection related to gallstones and requires further imaging.
details.reasoning: Ordering an abdominal ultrasound due to persistent abdominal discomfort, mild fever, and dark urine indicating a possible gallbladder issue. Found 4 relevant experiences suggesting ultrasound is key in differentiating gallstone-related complications.
details.learning_principles_used: Applied principle: Ultrasound is critical for diagnosing gallstone complications in similar cases.
details.viewer_output: Ordering abdominal ultrasound due to symptoms indicating potential gallbladder complications. Found 4 relevant past cases suggesting this approach.

timeline.summary: Abdominal ultrasound completed. Results will be discussed with your doctor.

details.test_outcome: Gallbladder wall thickness: 5.2 mm, Common bile duct diameter: 9.8 mm, Liver echogenicity: increased, Presence of biliary dilation: yes, No abnormalities in kidneys.
details.viewer_output: Abdominal ultrasound completed. Results will be discussed with your doctor.

timeline.summary: Ordering CT scan from CT Scan Room due to abnormal ultrasound findings indicating potential complications from gallstones. Found 3 relevant past cases suggesting this approach.

details.diagnosis: Acute Cholecystitis secondary to gallstones with possible infection
details.recommended_treatment: Cholecystectomy and initiation of IV antibiotics
details.context_for_next: Further imaging needed due to abnormal ultrasound findings, suggesting complications from gallstones.
details.ordered_test: CT scan from CT Scan Room
details.reasoning: CT scan is ordered to assess for potential complications related to gallstones and cholecystitis, following abnormal ultrasound findings. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes supporting this approach.
details.learning_principles_used: Used case #1: patient with abnormal ultrasound correlated with gallstones and successful intervention led to appropriate treatment.
details.viewer_output: Ordering CT scan from CT Scan Room due to abnormal ultrasound findings indicating potential complications from gallstones. Found 3 relevant past cases suggesting this approach.

timeline.summary: CT scan completed. Results will be discussed with your doctor.

details.test_outcome: Findings: 2.5 cm dilated common bile duct, intrahepatic bile duct dilation, pericholecystic fluid, and mild hepatic steatosis.
details.viewer_output: CT scan completed. Results will be discussed with your doctor.

timeline.summary: Diagnosis: Cholecystitis with potential biliary obstruction from gallstones. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: Surgical evaluation for possible cholecystectomy and management of biliary obstruction.

details.diagnosis: Cholecystitis with potential biliary obstruction from gallstones
details.recommended_treatment: Surgical evaluation for possible cholecystectomy and management of biliary obstruction.
details.context_for_next: Diagnosis based on ultrasound and CT scan findings indicating gallbladder complications and biliary dilation.
details.reasoning: Given abnormal ultrasound and CT findings of common bile duct dilation and pericholecystic fluid, the diagnosis of cholecystitis with potential biliary obstruction from gallstones is warranted. Three similar cases showed positive outcomes with surgical intervention in gallbladder-related issues. Confidence in the diagnosis is high due to concurrent imaging results.
details.learning_principles_used: Used case #1: Abnormal gallbladder ultrasound led to cholecystitis diagnosis; Applied principle: Surgical intervention is typically required in this scenario.
details.viewer_output: Diagnosis: Cholecystitis with potential biliary obstruction from gallstones. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: Surgical evaluation for possible cholecystectomy and management of biliary obstruction.

timeline.summary: Learned: Persistent abdominal discomfort, dark urine, and fever require thorough evaluation for cholangitis. This will improve future diagnoses of biliary tract infections.

details.principle_text: Persistent abdominal discomfort, dark urine, and mild fever require liver function tests and biliary imaging to rule out cholangitis.
details.reasoning: Initially diagnosed as cholecystitis without considering the signs of infection and biliary obstruction, which led to misdiagnosis. Ensuring that liver function tests and appropriate imaging are conducted can prevent overlooking cholangitis in similar presentations.
details.viewer_output: Learned: Persistent abdominal discomfort, dark urine, and fever require thorough evaluation for cholangitis. This will improve future diagnoses of biliary tract infections.
